system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

{"content": "     1|# 30VCS — 10 Page Strategy & Analysis
     2|
     3|## Brand Identity Analysis
     4|
     5|30VCS is a **cannabis community club** in Paraguay that blends:
     6|- **Culture**: Music, art, ferias, street parties
     7|- **Medicine**: Education, doctor access, patient support
     8|- **Activism**: Art 30 advocacy, community planting, policy reform
     9|- **Commerce**: Smokewear merch line
    10|
    11|Their current digital presence is 100% Instagram. The website needs to serve as the **central hub** that Instagram links to — events, merch, education, community.
    12|
    13|---
    14|
    15|## 10 Pages
    16|
    17|### 1. Home
    18|
    19|**Purpose**: First impression. Capture the energy of their events. Establish brand.
    20|
    21|**Sections**:
    22|- Full-screen hero with video (if available) or cinematic photo carousel from their events
    23|- Value props: 4 pillars (Comunidad, Cultura, Siembra, Medicina)
    24|- Upcoming event highlight (dynamic or manually updated)
    25|- Latest news/blog post teaser
    26|- Instagram feed embed (live)
    27|- CTA: Join the club / Follow on IG
    28|
    29|**Already built** on current site — needs polish (video support, smoother animations).
    30|
    31|---
    32|
    33|### 2. Nosotros (About)
    34|
    35|**Purpose**: Tell the story. Why 30VCS exists. The "30" meaning. Who runs it.
    36|
    37|**Sections**:
    38|- Origin story: born from Art 30 of Law 1340/88
    39|- Mission, Vision, Values
    40|- The meaning of VCS (needs client input)
    41|- Founder/organizer profiles (photos + short bios)
    42|- Timeline: first feria → siembra → x Sublime → future
    43|- Hemp Guarani / social impact connection
    44|
    45|---
    46|
    47|### 3. Eventos (Events)
    48|
    49|**Purpose**: Event calendar + ticket sales. The main community driver.
    50|
    51|**Sections**:
    52|- Upcoming events with date, time, location, ticket price
    53|- Past event galleries (Plaza Italia, Piko Studio, Areguá)
    54|- Event categories: Ferias, Fiestas, Siembras, Charlas
    55|- RSVP / ticket purchase flow (WhatsApp or MercadoPago)
    56|- Map embed for locations
    57|- Recurring event series (30VCS x Sublime, monthly ferias)
    58|
    59|**Revenue driver**: Event tickets, early-bird pricing, VIP options.
    60|
    61|---
    62|
    63|### 4. Smokewear (Merch Store)
    64|
    65|**Purpose**: E-commerce for the clothing line. Brand visibility + revenue.
    66|
    67|**Sections**:
    68|- Product catalog: remeras, buzos, gorras, stickers, lighters
    69|- Product photos (need client to send)
    70|- Sizes guide (talles)
    71|- WhatsApp checkout (simple) or MercadoPago (future)
    72|- Limited editions / drops
    73|- Shipping info (Asunción pickup + nationwide envío)
    74|
    75|**Revenue driver**: Direct merch sales.
    76|
    77|---
    78|
    79|### 5. Blog (Cannabis Education)
    80|
    81|**Purpose**: SEO traffic, thought leadership, community resource.
    82|
    83|**Content pillars**:
    84|- **Legales**: Guía del Art 30, cómo defenderte, cambios legislativos
    85|- **Medicinal**: Aceites, condiciones tratables, testimonios de pacientes, doctores aliados
    86|- **Cultura**: Historia del cannabis en Paraguay, prensado, ferias, música
    87|- **Cultivo**: Guías de siembra, temporada, tierra roja, plagas
    88|- **Actualidad**: Noticias del sector, leyes, eventos
    89|
    90|**SEO value**: High. Cannabis + Paraguay is an undersaturated keyword space.
    91|
    92|---
    93|
    94|### 6. Medicinal (Medical Cannabis Hub)
    95|
    96|**Purpose**: Serve the patient community. Differentiate from recreational-only clubs.
    97|
    98|**Sections**:
    99|- List of allied doctors (names, specialties, contact)
   100|- Conditions treatable with cannabis: epilepsia, dolor crónico, ansiedad, etc.
   101|- How to access CBD oil in Paraguay legally
   102|- Downloadable guides
   103|- Patient testimonials
   104|- FAQ: "¿El aceite es legal?", "¿Necesito receta?"
   105|
   106|**Social impact**: This positions 30VCS as serious and community-oriented, not just parties.
   107|
   108|---
   109|
   110|### 7. Cultivo (Cultivation Guide)
   111|
   112|**Purpose**: Community education + siembra event promotion.
   113|
   114|**Sections**:
   115|- Siembra comunitaria recap (Areguá photos)
   116|- Basic cultivation calendar for Paraguay (planting months, harvest)
   117|- Tierra roja: why Paraguay is ideal
   118|- Seed sources in PY
   119|- Legal limits (3 plants proposal, Art 30)
   120|- Upcoming planting events
   121|
   122|**Community value**: Farmers and cultivators are a core audience.
   123|
   124|---
   125|
   126|### 8. Galería (Media Gallery)
   127|
   128|**Purpose**: Show, don't tell. Event energy sells the club.
   129|
   130|**Sections**:
   131|- Photo galleries from each event
   132|- Video section (IG Reels embedded)
   133|- User-generated content (repost from community)
   134|- Brand photoshoots for Smokewear
   135|- Before/after of siembra events
   136|
   137|**Social proof**: Nothing convinces like seeing 200 people at Plaza Italia having a good time.
   138|
   139|---
   140|
   141|### 9. Únete (Join / Membership)
   142|
   143|**Purpose**: Grow the community. Build recurring revenue.
   144|
   145|**Sections**:
   146|- Free membership: newsletter + event notifications
   147|- Premium membership (future): descuentos en eventos, merch exclusivo, acceso a charlas
   148|- Volunteer signup: help with events, plantings, content
   149|- Partner/collaborator form: musicians, venues, doctors, growers
   150|- WhatsApp community link
   151|
   152|**Growth driver**: Converts IG followers → email subscribers → members.
   153|
   154|---
   155|
   156|### 10. Contacto
   157|
   158|**Purpose**: Partnerships, press, inquiries.
   159|
   160|**Sections**:
   161|- Instagram direct links (both accounts)
   162|- WhatsApp business number
   163|- Email contact form
   164|- Press kit download (logo, brand guidelines, photos)
   165|- FAQ (already exists on current site)
   166|- Location map (Asunción base)
   167|
   168|---
   169|
   170|## Features to Implement

   173||---|---------|------|----------|--------|
   174|| 1 | **Instagram Feed Embed** | Home, Galería | 🔴 High | 2h |

   189|## Content Hierarchy (What to Ask Client For)

   191|| Asset | Needed For | Status |
   192||-------|-----------|--------|
   193|| Logo (high res) | Branding | ❌ Missing |

   203|
   204|## Implementation Roadmap
   205|
   206|**Phase 1 (Week 1-2)**: Polish current MVP → Home + About + Events + Smokewear + FAQ
   207|**Phase 2 (Week 3-4)**: Blog + Galería + Medicinal content
   208|**Phase 3 (Week 5-6)**: Cultivo + Únete + E-commerce + Newsletter

I know enough from my earlier research. Let me give you a comprehensive analysis based on global cannabis club models.

**Reference models worldwide:**

**Spain (Barcelona) — Cannabis Social Clubs (CSCs)**
The gold standard. ~300 private clubs in BCN alone. Key features:
- Member-only access (keycard entry, referral system)
- Gated product catalog visible only to paid members
- Monthly member meetings & votes on strain purchases
- Private Telegram/WhatsApp groups per membership tier
- "Guest pass" system (members bring 1 guest max)
- Community cultivation plots visible via webcam (transparency)
- Annual member assembly with financial reporting

**US Dispensaries — Loyalty programs (best examples: Cookies, Jungle Boys, Stiiizy)**
- Points-based: Gs. 100 spent = 1 point, 50 points = Gs. 10.000 off
- Birthday freebie (pre-roll or edible)
- Early access to limited drops
- Strain reviews & rating system (community-driven)
- "$1 member Sundays" — small exclusive discount to drive weekly traffic
- Referral rewards: "Colega trae colega" — bring a friend, both get a sticker/descuento

**Canada LPs (Legal Producers — Canopy, Tilray, Hexo)**
- Subscription boxes (monthly surprise selection of 3-5 products)
- Access to member-only events (harvest dinners, private tours)
- Digital "digital passport" with QR for legal purchases
- Online community forums with verified medical patient badges

**What works for 30VCS specifically (Paraguay context):**

**Members-Only Pages:**
1. **El Jardín** (cultivo diary) — Private blog of their community grow. Photos, dates, strains. Only members can comment.
2. **La Cueva** — Members-only event announcements (24h before public). Exclusive party locations.
3. **Directorio Médico Privado** — Full list of allied doctors + contact info (gated for patient safety)
4. **Muro de Intercambio** — Bulletin board: seeds, clones, vaporizers, knowledge. Members only.

**Foro / Community Ideas:**
1. **Foro de Pacientes** — Peer support. "Mi condición, mi experiencia." Moderated by doctor allies.
2. **Intercambio de Puntas** — Cultivation tips exchange. "Cómo va tu planta esta semana" — photo threads.
3. **Marketplace entre Miembros** — Buy/sell used equipment among verified members (vaporizers, grinders, lights).
4. **Cartelera de Eventos Propios** — Members can post their own meetups. "Llevo un parlante, juntada en Ñu Guasu".

**Other High-Value Features:**
1. **Digital Membership Card** — WhatsApp delivers a QR. Scan at events. Tracks attendance → loyalty points.
2. **Siembra Sponsor** — "Adoptá una planta" in the community grow. Recibís fotos semanales + cosecha share.
3. **Member of the Month** — Spotlight on a community member. Featured on IG + site. Free Smokewear item.
4. **Event Token System** — Attend 3 events → free merch. Scan QR at check-in.
5. **Anonymous Q&A Board** — Members submit questions about med use, cultivation, legal. Club answers publicly (anonymized).
6. **Voting Rights** — Members vote on: next event location, discount products, community initiative. Builds ownership.

**Implementation Order for 30VCS:**

| Phase | Feature | Why first |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| 1 | WhatsApp-delivered digital membership card + QR | Zero dev, instant trust |
| 2 | Members-only event early access | Drives signups |
| 3 | Telegram VIP group (gated) | Community instantly |
| 4 | Private Medical Directory | Real value + safety |
| 5 | Foro (Forum) — starts as Telegram thread, then web | Low friction |

The absolute easiest win: **WhatsApp-based membership**. No app. No login system. User sends "QUIERO SER MIEMBRO" → gets a manual or auto-replied QR code → shows at events. Track attendance via Google Sheets. Upgrade to web-based system only when they hit 200+ paying members.

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

build a service site - build a website for a client - audit my store - roast my site - fix ecommerce issues - improve my store - deploy ecommerce - new client site - create a business plan - research a business - lead to site - beauty wellness website - generate website from leads - deploy multiple sites - paragu-ai-leads - paralell site deployment --- # Standalone E-commerce Store Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 e-commerce stores. ## Architecture Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) -> VPS -> Traefik -> service_web:3000 ## Tech Stack Next.js 15 (standalone output), React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Docker + Swarm, Traefik, SQLite (optional) ## Build & Deploy ``` npm run build docker build -t :prod . docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml ``` ## Page Structure Home, Tienda, Producto/[slug], Envio, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Terminos, Nosotros, Promociones, Blog (posts + categories + [slug]), Sitemap, Error, Not-found, RSS ## Product Data Structure (Retail) Each product should carry rich spec data, not just name+price: ```json { "slug": "titanium", "name": "Titanium", "line": "Premium", "category": "Resorte", "price": "Gs. 1.800.000", "priceFrom": "2 plazas 140x190", "image": "/images/products/titanium.png", "description": "Premium con Euro Pillow Top.", "sizes": ["2 plazas 140x190: Gs. 1.800.000", "Queen 160x200: Gs. 2.200.000"], "firmness": "Firme", "warranty": "5 años", "weight": "Hasta 120 kg por lado", "colors": ["Gris", "Azul"], "badges": ["Pillow Top", "Antiácaros", "Más vendido"] } ``` Product pages render a specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), a measure/price table, and badges. ## Retail-Specific Features - **Bundles/Combos**: Section on homepage showing 2-3 product bundles with original price, bundle price, savings, and WhatsApp inquiry link - **Promo banners**: Downloadable from client's current site, displayed as clickable 3-column grid in `#0F1624` section - **Store locator**: GPS coordinates per store, clickable address → Google Maps, clickable phone → tel: link, type badges (Store/Mall/Outlet/Distribución), Argentina subsidiary section - **Product page**: Specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), size/price table, badges row - **Cross-sell**: "Complete your bundle" section — mattress + base + protector + pillows ## Critical Patterns - WhatsApp-first commerce (all CTAs go to WhatsApp) - Content-driven from content/es.json - Age-gated content (localStorage with 7-day timestamp expiry) - Discreet shipping guarantee (for adult stores) ## Content Structure content/es.json sections: siteConfig, navigation, home, products (14+ with id/slug/name/price/level/sound/safety fields), categories, faq, blog, footer, shipping, payments, socialProof, brand --- ## Comprehensive Site Audit Checklist Run on EVERY page. Be brutal. ### Audit Dimensions: 1. Visual/Design -- spacing, alignment, color, images, fonts, hierarchy, consistency 2. UX/Usability -- navigation, button placement, mobile, loading states, forms, click targets 3. Content -- copy quality, tone, grammar, completeness, placeholders, length 4. E-commerce -- conversion flow, trust signals, pricing, urgency, social proof, stock 5. Technical -- load speed, console errors, broken links, SEO, titles, structured data, a11y 6. Mobile -- viewport, tap targets, readability, nav overlap 7. Industry-specific -- privacy signals, discretion tone, age gate, comfort for first-timers 8. Cross-cutting -- image placeholders, inconsistent icons, duplication, text bugs, keyboard nav ### Severity Triage: - CRITICAL -- Broken functionality, legal exposure, blank pages. Fix immediately. - HIGH -- Trust-killing issues (no contact info, no product images, duplicate nav). Fix this batch. - MEDIUM -- UX friction, redundant elements. Fix alongside HIGH. - LOW -- Polish, accessibility extras. Fix when time permits. ### Batch Fix Strategy: 1. Fix all CRITICAL first (1-3 line fixes) 2. Fix all HIGH second (users notice these) 3. Fix MEDIUM alongside HIGH 4. Fix LOW last 5. Commit + deploy as single atomic batch ### Overview of this session (Fun4Me full upgrade) This session built on a basic skeleton store (Fun4Me, adult toys, Paraguay) and transformed it into a full-featured store matching the production El Viajero store. 25+ batches across: content overhaul, 15 pages, full search/filter/sort/pagination, adult-store privacy features (quick exit, privacy mode blur, anonymous reviews), WhatsApp commerce features, carousels, dark/light mode, PWA, structured data, SVG icons, loading states, product comparison, shipping calculator, product quiz, social proof, per-product FAQ, safety badges, sound level indicators, and a complete 520-item feature inventory. Then ran a brutal full-site roast across all pages and fixed all 21 identified issues in a single follow-up pass. ## Brand/Creative Client Sites (Band, Artist, Portfolio) When the client is a band, artist, or creative brand (no products, no services catalog), the e-commerce template is wrong. Build custom pages. See `references/band-site-pattern.md` for the full pattern — content schema, page component structure, conversion from monorepo, Spotify embed, and common pitfalls. ### Personal Data Stripping for Artist Sites from Personal Repos When building a public artist/band site from personal content (psychology repos, journals, letters): **MUST strip before publishing:** - Real names (family, partners, your own full name) - Specific medical/health disclosures (HIV, medications, treatment details) - Family role descriptions ("The Engineer", "child who wanted to be held" → "heart that wanted to be held") - Place-specific references ("in my own home" → "in the peripheries") - Specific biographical facts that could identify the person - Age, location, employer, educational details **Rewrite strategy:** - Replace specific disclosures with universal emotional truths - "I am not a risk / I am not a disease" → "I am not my past / I am not my shame" - "My family didn't know what to do with me" → "I learned young that feelings were a dangerous thing" - Keep the emotional core intact while removing identifying specifics **Checklist:** run `grep -rni 'family|parents|mother|father|hiv|positive|diagnos|weiss|real name'` on all site files before deploy. ### Songs/Lyrics Content Pattern For artist sites with original music, use a `src/data/songs.ts` data module instead of es.json: ```ts export const songs = [ { id: 'song-slug', title: 'Song Title', subtitle: 'One-line hook or tagline', lyrics: `Full lyrics as template literal`, genre: 'Alternative', duration: '4:12', mood: 'Vulnerable, building to powerful' } ] ``` Then render an **accordion/expandable lyrics section** — clicking a song title expands to show full lyrics beneath it. Use `useState` to track which song is open. ### PAB Token Bridge for Artist Sites For sites following the Paragu-ai Builder design token pattern, create `src/tokens/brand.tokens.json`: ```json { "colors": { "primary": { "value": "#D4956A", "type": "color" }, "background": { "value": "#0D0D14", "type": "color" }, "surface": { "value": "#1C1C2A", "type": "color" } }, "typography": { "fontFamily": { "heading": { "value": "'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif", "type": "fontFamily" }, "body": { "value": "'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif", "type": "fontFamily" }, "accent": { "value": "'Cormorant Garamond', Georgia, serif", "type": "fontFamily" } } } } ``` Map tokens to CSS variables in `globals.css`: ```css @theme { --color-primary: #D4956A; --color-background: #0D0D14; --color-surface: #1C1C2A; } ``` ### Social Icons Row Artist sites should include a social icons row at the bottom of the contact section with Instagram, YouTube, and Spotify SVG icons. Use `hover:text-[color]` transitions on each to match the platform brand color. ### Page Structure for Band Sites - **Home**: Hero headline/subheadline, bio section, feature cards (discography, shows, members), Spotify embed iframe, discography cards with album art, team grid (members + role + bio), testimonials, newsletter signup, booking CTA - **Música**: Full discography with album art per item (from Spotify CDN), track listing per album, Spotify embed - **Videos**: YouTube embed cards — extract video ID from URL with regex `/watch\?v=([^&]+)/` - **Letras**: Song title + album metadata, lyrics displayed as lines in a styled dark box - **Shows**: Event cards with date, venue, description — Calendar icon per card - **Galería**: Photo grid with category filter buttons (Todas / En Vivo / Banda / Festivales), hover overlay on images - **Contacto**: Contact cards for each channel (WhatsApp, Email, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, Spotify) — each with icon + label + hover color matching the platform brand ### Content Structure (es.json) ```json { "siteName": "Nüdo", "tagline": "Hardcore Metal desde Capiatá, Paraguay", "contact": { "email", "instagram", "facebook", "spotify", "youtube" }, "navigation": { "items": [{ "label", "href" }], "ctaText": "Escuchanos en Spotify", "ctaHref": "..." }, "home": { "hero": { "headline", "subheadline", "ctaPrimaryText/Href", "ctaSecondaryText/Href", "trustBadges" }, "bio", "features" }, "team": [{ "name", "role", "bio" }], "discography": [{ "title", "year", "type", "label", "imageUrl" (spotify CDN), "spotifyUrl", "tracks": [] }], "lyrics": [{ "title", "album", "lyrics": [line, line, ...] }], "shows": [{ "title", "date", "venue", "description" }], "gallery": [{ "src", "alt", "category" }], "videos": [{ "title", "url", "thumbnail" }], "testimonials": [{ "quote", "author" }], "newsletter": { "title", "subtitle", "placeholder", "submitLabel" }, "footer": { "copyright" }, "seo": { "defaultTitle", "defaultDescription", "ogImage" } } ``` ### Visual Identity for Band Sites - **Dark theme**: `#0d0d0d` background, `#1a1a1a` sections, `#222` cards, `#333` borders - **Primary accent**: `#8B0000` (dark red) for CTAs and highlights, hover `#B22222` - **Text**: `#f5f5f5` foreground, `#a0a0a0` muted, `#666` metadata - **Typography**: uppercase tracking-wider on metadata, all-caps navigation, impactful headings - **Section gradients**: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0d0d0d 0%, #1a0000 50%, #0d0d0d 100%)` for hero sections - **Spotify embed**: 352px height iframe, centered section with title + caption ### Docker: No public/ dir Edge Case Band sites often have no `public/` directory. Remove the `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` line from Dockerfile to avoid build failure ("/app/public: not found"). ### Deployment Same as standalone store: build → deploy via Docker Swarm + Traefik. DNS A record to VPS IP, Cloudflare proxied. --- ## Lead-to-Site Pipeline (Beauty/Wellness Template) When generating sites from lead data (e.g., `paragu-ai-leads` repo), use the generator script + patterns in `references/lead-to-site-pipeline.md`. Key differences from e-commerce stores: - **Pages**: Home, Servicios, Galería, Equipo, Contacto (no Tienda/Producto pages) - **Content**: Services grid with name/price/desc/duration per category - **Nav labels**: vary by business type (peluquería → "Servicios", gimnasio → "Planes") - **Palettes**: Elegant Rose (peluquería), Deep Navy+Gold (spa), Dark+Red (gimnasio) - **Dockerfile MANDATORY**: `output: "standalone"` in next.config.ts or multi-stage build fails ## Auth System: Client → Real API Wiring When upgrading from localStorage-only auth to real API-backed auth: ### SQLite Schema (must include sessions table) The schema in `lib/db.ts` needs a `sessions` table for token-based auth:``` ## Lead-to-Site Batch Deploy Script See `scripts/deploy-lead-site.sh` — reusable script for deploying a single lead site to VPS. Run it individually per site (not in a loop) to avoid SSH timeout on long builds: ```sql CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions ( token TEXT PRIMARY KEY, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, expires_at TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ); ``` ### Auth context patterns 1. **Always wrap children in AuthContext.Provider** — never conditionally render a fallback during loading/SSR. Children will try to call `useAuth()` and crash. Set all initial state to empty/null values. 2. **Guard localStorage** — check `typeof window !== "undefined"` before any `localStorage.getItem()` call. SSR runs this code. 3. **API helper with bearer token** — define a helper function that reads token from localStorage and attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header. This avoids repeating the pattern in every method. 4. **Auth routes pattern** — single POST /api/auth route with `action` field dispatching to login/register/me/logout branches. Rate limit with in-memory map keyed on `email:ip`. 5. **Addresses/Orders as separate routes** — PUT/DELETE on /api/addresses for CRUD, GET/POST on /api/orders. Authenticate by reading session token from `Authorization` header. 6. **Dynamic export** — all pages that use `useAuth` need `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` to prevent Next.js 15 from trying to prerender them during build (will crash with "useAuth must be used within AuthProvider"). ## Docker Build: Typescript devDep Problem The base image `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` has `ENV NODE_ENV=production`. This causes `npm install` in the deps stage to skip devDependencies including `typescript`, which Next.js 15 needs to parse `next.config.ts`. Fix: Set `ENV NODE_ENV=development` in the deps stage: ```dockerfile # MANDATORY: next.config.ts must have output: "standalone" # Without this, .next/standalone/server.js won't exist and container crashes on start FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=development COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./ RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps ``` Keep the builder stage at `NODE_ENV=production` (devDeps already installed by deps stage). ### Cloudflare SSL Mode for New Subdomains New proxied (orange cloud) subdomains default to **Full (Strict)** mode in Cloudflare. This rejects self-signed origin certs with error 526. Fix: In Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Overview, change from "Full (Strict)" to **"Full"** (accepts any origin cert). Or generate a Cloudflare Origin CA cert. ACME HTTP-01 challenge does NOT work through Cloudflare proxy — Let's Encrypt hits CF edge IPs, not the VPS. Use DNS challenge or temporary DNS-only mode for ACME. ### Batch Site Generation from Leads See `references/python-site-generator-pattern.md` for the Python script pattern that generates 5+ structurally-identical sites from data dicts. Use when deploying multiple leads from `paragu-ai-leads` repo. ### Lead Site Productionizing (Auto-Generated Site Upgrade) When deploying structurally-identical lead sites from a template (e.g., paragu-ai-leads), they often ship with: - Only 3 pages (/, /contacto, /servicios) - Missing HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 in Dockerfile → 504 errors - Missing traefik.docker.network label → Traefik can't route - No healthcheck, no non-root user - No security headers, no robots/sitemap - No about, FAQ, privacy, terms, 404, error pages - Scissors icon on all service sections (template leftover even for gyms/spas) - Generic "Transformamos tu estilo" tagline copied across all business types **Upgrade checklist for each lead site:** see `references/lead-site-deployment-checklist.md`. Pattern: same as productionizing any MVP site — add pages + Docker hardening + SEO + content localization. ## Common Pitfalls: ### Deep Business Research Before building any client site, run the full research pipeline — see `references/deep-business-research.md`. This includes: scraping competitor sites for all images, mapping GPS coordinates, identifying related companies/subsidiaries, finding director/owner names via press interviews, and documenting cross-sell opportunities. ### Promo carousels: badge + title + description - Blog category filtering: accented chars need NFD normalization before comparison - Dynamic page titles: reset on unmount to prevent stale titles - Social proof popups: position above WhatsApp float (bottom-32) - Age gates: use timestamp-based expiry, not boolean localStorage flags - Stats: initialize with "---" not "0" to avoid flash before animation - Accessibility: add skip-to-content link with #main-content target - Filter pagination: reset page to 1 when any filter changes - Product images: SVG files in /public/images/ exist but es.json must reference them by correct path. "✦" placeholder means the image path is wrong or missing. - Footer social dedup: if social bar (icon links) AND footer column (text links) both point to same Insta/WA, remove the text link column. - Title template in layout.tsx shows default even with dynamic page titles -- client components set document.title in useEffect, but SSR output still shows the default until hydration. - Price range sliders: two stacked range inputs overlap visually when values are close. Use a single range input (max price only) for simplicity. - Anon review forms: must have `formRef.current?.reset()` + setRating state on submit. Interactive stars need `onClick` handler and conditional `text-warning`/`text-border` classes. - Accented category names: URLs with accents don't match NFD-normalized comparisons. Always strip combining diacritics when generating URL slugs. - Guest mode banner: use localStorage with 24h timestamp expiry, not sessionStorage (which shows on every new tab) or simple boolean (which never shows again). - Page metadata/layout.tsx: the `title` export in metadata is SSR-only. Client-side dynamic titles need a separate `useEffect` with cleanup. - SVG images on product cards: the SafeImage component renders `` tags. Give the container `class="overflow-hidden"` so the SVG doesn't overflow the gradient background. - Filter sidebar scroll position: when toggling filters on/off, the sidebar scrolls to top. Acceptable limitation unless building a custom scroll-position cache. ### Roast Process (Post-Deploy Audit) After any build or upgrade, run this systematic page-by-page audit. The full 18-point checklist from the Fun4Me session: 1. **Promo carousel**: badge + title + description rendered as one word? Badge needs `mr-1.5`, description needs `ml-2`, title needs proper whitespace. 2. **Blog content**: is the full article content rendering, or just the excerpt? If content lives in es.json, check it's long enough. If stored in a code map, verify all slugs have entries. 3. **Blog WhatsApp URL**: avoid literal template strings (`${encodeURIComponent(post.title)}` in href). Must use proper JS interpolation. 4. **Stats animation**: initial state is "0" or "—"? "0" causes flash of broken numbers. Use "—". 5. **Age gate**: boolean flag or timestamp? Timestamp with 7-day expiry is more resilient. 6. **Social proof popup**: does it overlap the WhatsApp float? Position at `bottom-32` or lower. 7. **Footer**: social platform links appear in both a column AND an icon bar? Remove the text-link column. 8. **Blog category URLs**: accented chars (Guías → guias)? Must NFD-normalize before filter comparison. 9. **Contact page**: has hours, address, multiple topic-specific buttons? Not just a single WhatsApp link. 10. **Privacy/Terms pages**: 8+ substantive sections, not 3-4 one-liners. 11. **Product images**: "✦" anywhere on product cards = image path broken or missing. SVGs should render. 12. **Page titles**: browser tab shows product name, not just site default. Check with `document.title` after hydration. 13. **Breadcrumbs**: visible on product/detail pages, not just hidden JSON-LD in ``. 14. **Accessibility**: skip-to-content link present? `#main-content` target exists? Dark mode toggle has correct `aria-label`? 15. **Duplicate badges**: "Principiante" showing in both LevelBadge and SafetyBadges? Only one should show it. 16. **Price slider**: dual range inputs overlap visually? Replace with single max-price slider. 17. **Anon review form**: reset on submit? Stars are clickable? Form resets with `formRef.current?.reset()`. 18. **Guest mode banner**: shows every new tab (sessionStorage)? Use localStorage with 24h timestamp instead. The `standalone-ecommerce-store` skill is comprehensive for e-commerce and band/artist sites. But this 30VCS session introduced a **new class of site**: the **cannabis community club / membership site** — which has completely different patterns from e-commerce or brand/portfolio sites. This is a distinct class that should be its own reference.

Key novel patterns from this session that aren't in any existing skill:
- WhatsApp-based membership (no auth system, no user database)
- Event RSVP + QR ticket system
- Content-driven multi-page architecture (all 26 pages from es.json)

# Community Club Website

Build content-driven community/club websites with WhatsApp-based membership, private member pages, forums, events, loyalty points, voting, and gated content. Zero login system needed — all flows go through WhatsApp.

## Architecture

Cloudflare (DNS, SSL, proxied) → VPS → Docker Swarm → Traefik → next.js:3000

## Tech Stack

Next.js 16 standalone, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Docker + Swarm, Traefik, content/es.json

## Core Concept: WhatsApp-Based Membership

This is the key innovation — **no user database, no auth system, no login page**. Members join via WhatsApp and receive a digital membership card with QR. Event attendance is tracked by QR scan (manually or via Google Sheets). The website serves as the public hub + private content gallery, while WhatsApp handles the transactional layer.

**Why this works for clubs in developing countries:**
- Zero friction (everyone has WhatsApp)
- No password reset, no email verification, no app install
- Trust-based community management
- Easy to start with Google Sheets, upgrade to real backend later

## Page Architecture

### Public Pages (10)
- / — Home with hero, pillars, features grid, event preview, IG link
- /nosotros — Origin story, timeline, mission/vision
- /eventos — Upcoming events with pricing + past event gallery
- /smokewear — Product catalog with WhatsApp checkout
- /blog — Content categories with color-coded tags
- /medicinal — Medical conditions, access guide, disclaimer
- /cultivo — Cultivation guides + community planting events
- /galeria — Photo albums with links to Instagram
- /unete — Ways to join (follow on IG, attend, volunteer, sponsor)
- /contacto — Contact channels + form that sends to WhatsApp

### Membership Hub (11 pages under /miembros/)
- /miembros — Membership tiers, how it works, card preview, points system
- /miembros/tarjeta — Digital membership card with QR
- /miembros/puntos — Loyalty points: earn rules + rewards
- /miembros/eventos — Attendance history + upcoming events
- /miembros/descuentos — Active perks by tier
- /miembros/el-jardin — Private cultivation diary (weekly entries)
- /miembros/la-cueva — Secret/private events for members
- /miembros/directorio — Ally doctors directory (gated)
- /miembros/muro — Bulletin board categories (Telegram-based)
- /miembros/foro — Discussion threads with category badges
- /miembros/votaciones — Active polls + past results
- /miembros/preguntas-anonimas — Anonymous Q&A with club answers
- /miembros/sponsor-planta — Sponsor-a-plant crowdfunding tiers

## Content Structure (content/es.json)

The entire site is driven from a single JSON file. Structure:

```json
{
  "site": { "name", "tagline", "description", "url", "whatsapp", "instagram", "instagram2", "email" },
  "nav": { "links": [{ "label", "href" }] },
  "home": { "hero", "pillars", "features", "faq" },
  "nosotros": { "hero", "intro", "mission", "vision", "history", "team" },
  "eventos": { "hero", "upcoming": [{ "title", "date", "time", "location", "description", "price", "link", "tag" }], "past": [...] },
  "smokewear": { "hero", "intro", "products": [{ "name", "price", "desc", "sizes" }] },
  "blog": { "hero", "posts": [{ "title", "date", "category", "excerpt", "readTime" }] },
  "medicinal": { "hero", "intro", "conditions": [{ "name", "desc" }], "steps": [...], "disclaimer" },
  "cultivo": { "hero", "intro", "guides": [...], "plantingEvents": [...] },
  "galeria": { "hero", "albums": [...] },

```

## Key Implementation Patterns

### Header with Dropdown
For clubs with 10+ nav links, show first 5 in desktop nav and put the rest in a hover dropdown labeled "Más ▾". Mobile gets a hamburger with all links.

### Gold/Members Link Emphasis
The "Miembros" link gets gold color + bolder font to stand out. Use a `gold: true` property on the nav link object.

### Member Sub-navigation
The /miembros/ layout has a sub-nav bar below the main header showing links to all member pages. This is in a `layout.tsx` in the `/miembros/` directory.

### Digital Membership Card
Premium-looking card with gradient background (dark green/gold), tier badge, member name, ID, and QR code placeholder. Rendered as static HTML (no real QR generation — placeholder SVG). WhatsApp delivers the actual card image.

### Points System Display
Two sections: "Ganá puntos" (actions → +points) and "Canjeá premios" (points → reward). Displayed as alternating rows with gold/green highlights.

### Event RSVP
All CTA buttons go to WhatsApp with pre-filled message. No form processing.

### Anonymous Q&A
Display as question/answer cards with ❓/💬 icons. The "ask" button opens WhatsApp with pre-filled message.

### Voting
Radio buttons for active polls (visual only — votes are counted externally). Past results displayed as verified list.

### Forum Threads
Category color-coded badges. Stats row: author, replies count, last activity time.

### Sponsor-a-Plant Grid
Two columns: "Semilla" (Gs. 50K) and "Bosque" (Gs. 100K). Perks listed with checkmarks. WhatsApp CTA.

## Build & Deploy

```bash
cd /root/club-site
npm run build
docker build -t club-site:latest .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml club-site
```

## Color Theme (Cannabis Club)
- Background: #0d0d0d
- Primary green: #1a3a2a (--color-primary)
- Secondary gold: #d4a84b (--color-secondary)
- Surface: #1a1a1a
- Card border: rgba(255,255,255,0.06)
- Glass card: rgba(255,255,255,0.03) + blur(16px)
- CTA green: #2d7d46
- Accent red: #e63946

The gold gradient: `linear-gradient(135deg, #d4a84b, #f0d68a)`

## Pitfalls

1. **Massive es.json file**: With 26 pages and membership data, the JSON file grows to 15K+. Use modular imports or split into /content/pages/ if it exceeds 20K.
2. **WhatsApp placeholder number**: Always use a real number or clear placeholder. Building with "****" causes confusion.
3. **Client needs to provide**: What VCS means, founder names, product photos, doctor contacts, real WhatsApp number. Get these before launch.
4. **QR code generation**: The static site generates a QR placeholder SVG. For production, either generate real QR codes at build time (qrcode npm package) or have the club send them manually via WhatsApp.
5. **No real forum backend**: The forum threads are static demo data. A real forum needs a backend or Telegram integration. Document this as a Phase 2 upgrade path.
6. **Member-only gating**: The "private" pages are publicly accessible at their URLs since there's no auth system. This works for a club's early stage (trust-based). For real gating, add Vercel/Netlify password protection or a simple PIN gate.
7. **Event attendance tracking**: QR in the card is a placeholder. For actual attendance tracking, either use Google Sheets + manual scan or integrate a QR scanner (Phase 2).
8. **Gold tier voting**: Only Bosque members can vote. This is an honor system without real auth — declared on the page text, not enforced technically.
9. **Translation**: All content is in Spanish (es.json). For multilingual clubs, split into es.json, en.json, etc.
10. **Image handling**: Without real event photos, use emoji placeholders (📸, 👕). The galería pages show album descriptions but no actual images.

{"action": "write_file", "file_content": "# Global Cannabis Club Patterns Research

Compiled from building the 30VCS (Paraguay cannabis community club) website. These patterns apply to clubs worldwide — adapt for local context.

## Reference Models

### Spain — Cannabis Social Clubs (CSCs) — Gold Standard
300+ private clubs in Barcelona alone. Key mechanics:
- Member-only access (keycard entry, referral system)
- Gated product catalog visible only to paid members
- Monthly member meetings & votes on strain purchases
- Private Telegram/WhatsApp groups per membership tier
- "Guest pass" system (members bring 1 guest max)
- Community cultivation plots visible via webcam (transparency)
- Annual member assembly with financial reporting

### US Dispensaries — Loyalty Programs
Best examples: Cookies, Jungle Boys, Stiiizy
- Points-based: every Gs. 100 spent = 1 point, 50 points = Gs. 10.000 off
- Birthday freebie (pre-roll or edible)
- Early access to limited drops
- Strain reviews & rating system (community-driven)
- "$1 member Sundays" — small exclusive discount to drive weekly traffic
- Referral rewards: bring a friend, both get a sticker/descuento

### Canada LPs (Canopy, Tilray, Hexo)
- Subscription boxes (monthly surprise selection of 3-5 products)
- Access to member-only events (harvest dinners, private tours)
- Digital "digital passport" with QR for legal purchases
- Online community forums with verified medical patient badges

## Membership Tier Design for LATAM Clubs

Adapted from the Spanish CSC + US loyalty models for Paraguay context:

| Tier | Price | Trigger Purchase | Key Perks |
|------|-------|-----------------|-----------|
| Semilla | Free | Newsletter signup | Monthly newsletter, event notifications, 5% merch discount, public forum access |
| Planta | Gs. 30K/mo (~$4) | WhatsApp message | All Semilla + 48h event early access, 10% merch, private cultivation diary, digital QR card, Telegram VIP group |
| Bosque | Gs. 70K/mo (~$9.50) | WhatsApp message | All Planta + free events, 20% merch, cultivation kit gift, monthly raffle, voting rights, plant sponsorship |

Price points are designed to be:
- Semilla: zero barrier, captures email/WA for retargeting
- Planta: price of 1 beer per week, feels like nothing
- Bosque: price of a lunch out, feels like real commitment

## WhatsApp Membership Flow (No App, No Login)

The entire membership pipeline runs on WhatsApp:

1. User clicks "Unirse" on website → opens WhatsApp with pre-filled message "Quiero ser miembro [Tier]"
2. Club admin responds manually (early stage) or auto-replies (scalable) with:
   - Welcome message & tier perks summary
   - Payment info (transferencia bancaria, billetera, efectivo)
   - Request for name + preferred alias
3. On payment confirmation, admin sends:
   - Digital membership card image (QR code + name + tier + ID)
   - Telegram group invite link (for Planta/Bosque)
   - Next event info
4. Member shows card QR at events → admin marks attendance in Google Sheets

This handles everyone from 0 to 200 members with no backend engineering.

## Private Member Pages That Work

Based on analysis of which gated content drives real engagement:

1. **El Jardín (Cultivation Diary)** — Weekly photo updates of community grow. High engagement because members watch "their" plants grow. Make entries personal: date, strain name, height, notes, photos.
2. **La Cueva (Secret Events)** — FOMO driver. Announcements of invite-only after-parties, workshops, intimate gatherings. Location revealed only 24h before.
3. **Directorio Médico (Medical Directory)** — Real value for patients. Reassurance that the club works with real doctors. Gated because doctor contact info should not be public.
4. **Muro de Intercambio (Bulletin Board)** — User-generated content. Members can trade seeds/clones/equipment, share tips, organize meetups. Works best via Telegram.
5. **Foro (Discussion Forum)** — Six topic categories: Cultivo, Medicina, Comunidad, Legal, Equipamiento. Generate 6-10 starter threads to seed discussion.

## Revenue Drivers for Clubs

| Feature | Revenue Model | Difficulty |
|---------|--------------|------------|
| Tiered membership | Monthly recurring | Low (WhatsApp) |
| Event tickets | Per-event | Low (WA payment) |
| Merch/Smokewear | Retail margin | Low (WA checkout) |
| Sponsor-a-plant | Monthly micro-donation | Low |
| Event VIP passes | Premium upcharge | Low |
| Raffles/sorteos | Add-on | Medium |
| Dr. consultation fee split | Commission per patient | Medium |
| Brand sponsorships (strains, etc.) | Monthly from brands | Medium |

## Global Club Names (Inspiration)
- "La Cueva" — secret events (30VCS)
- "El Jardín" — cultivation diary (30VCS)
- "Pico Studio" — event venue (30VCS x Sublime)
- "La Mesa" — shared consumption area
- "El Invernadero" — grow space
- "La Sala" — lounge
- "El Árbol" — membership referral tree

## Implementation Order for Minimum Viable Club Site

| Phase | What | Why |
|-------|------|-----|
| 0 | Static site with 10 public pages + membership tiers | Establishes online presence |
| 1 | WhatsApp-delivered digital membership card + QR | Zero dev, instant trust |
| 2 | Members-only event early access landing page | Drives signups |
| 3 | Telegram VIP group (gated per tier) | Community instantly |
| 4 | Private medical directory page (gated) | Real value + safety |
| 5 | Forum — starts as Telegram thread, then migrates to web-based | Low friction start |

## Legal Caveats for Cannabis Clubs

- In Paraguay: Art 30 of Law 1340/88 despenalizes possession of up to 10g. Medical cannabis legal with prescription. Cultivation limits unclear (~3 plants considered personal use).
- In Spain: CSCs operate in legal gray zone. Member-only, non-profit, closed-loop production.
- Never: sell cannabis directly on the website. The club model is about community, education, and culture.
- Always: include medical disclaimer on all medicinal content. Disclaimer: "Esta información es educativa y no reemplaza una consulta médica."
